title: Logging Pipeline and Debug Service Flow
interaction_flow: Caller → LoggerFacade: .fields({ category, ...extras }) → returns new LoggerFacade
LoggerFacade → FieldsLogger: new FieldsLogger({ fields, delegate: this.impl })
LoggerFacade: wraps new FieldsLogger as impl in new LoggerFacade instance
FieldsLogger.log(level, message, fields, values) → merges Object.assign({}, this.fields, fields)
PrefixLogger.log(level, message, fields, values) → prepends this.prefix to message
CategorizedToggleLogger → delegate.log(level, message, fields, values) [if category enabled]
ConsoleLogger: builds ANSI-colored string with [LEVEL] prefix
ConsoleLogger: appends key=value pairs from fields
Debug → puter.logger (SimpleLogger): .on(category) for each param entry
Parent Window → Debug: postMessage({ $:'puterjs-debug', cmd:'log.on', category })
svc_exec: calls provider fn → returns { enabled_logs: 'cat1;cat2' } when categories set
Puter constructor → SimpleLogger: new SimpleLogger() → assigned to this.logger
After whoami resolves → logger.fields({ prefix: '[appName]' }) → new SimpleLogger with prefix field
key_relationships: LoggerFacade delegates log calls to impl (ConsoleLogger | FieldsLogger | CategorizedToggleLogger | ToggleLogger | PrefixLogger)
FieldsLogger wraps delegate and merges default fields
PrefixLogger wraps delegate and prepends prefix string to message
CategorizedToggleLogger wraps delegate; gates on fields.category membership in this.categories
ToggleLogger wraps delegate; global boolean gate
ConsoleLogger is terminal node — writes to console with ANSI color per log level
ArrayLogger is terminal node — stores entries in buffer[] (test/inspection use)
DebugService (GUI) controls puter.logger.on/off and propagates via postMessage to iframes
Debug module (puter-js) consumes 'enabled_logs' URL param and listens for postMessage from parent
SimpleLogger (puter-js/index.js) is a local implementation: Set-based .enabled, .on(cat), .fields() chaining
svc_exec receives enabled_logs as query param from DebugService param provider

methods: log(level, message, fields, values) → delegate.log with merged fields
log(level, message, fields, values) → delegate.log(level, prefix+message, ...)
log(level, message, fields, values) — checks fields.category
log(level, message, fields, values) — passes through if enabled
log(level, message, fields, values) — ANSI colored output
logs(category) — enables category, broadcasts to iframes
_init() — reads URL params, sets up postMessage listener

important_notes: Decorator chain pattern: LoggerFacade → FieldsLogger → CategorizedToggleLogger → ConsoleLogger
CategorizedToggleLogger gates messages by fields.category — message is silently dropped if category not enabled
DebugService.logs(category) is a GUI-side control that simultaneously enables category locally (puter.logger.on) AND propagates to all embedded iframes via postMessage
Debug module (puter-js) parses 'enabled_logs' URL query param (semicolon-delimited) on init for programmatic toggle
svc_exec param provider injects enabled_logs into app exec params so child iframes inherit active log categories
Backend log.js (stringify_log_entry) is separate from putility log.js — backend handles timestamp formatting, JSON coloring, and 'crumbs' breadcrumb context; not part of the putility TLogger chain
SimpleLogger in puter-js/index.js is a standalone class (not from putility) with subset of same API; used as puter.logger before putility-based logger is available
LoggerFacade.cat must be provided if using on()/off() — it must be a CategorizedToggleLogger in the impl chain
ArrayLogger is used only in tests to capture log entries in buffer[]
